entry.title: Build a Solid Security Foundation on SAP Business Technology Platform - New E-Learning Now Live
entry.content:
For security specialists and SAP BTP practitioners, the ability to protect critical systems and data is more essential than ever. That’s why we’re excited to introduce our new e-learning course: SAP Business Technology Platform Security Fundamentals.

This course provides the key concepts, practical insights, and technical guidance you need to design and maintain a secure SAP BTP environment. You’ll gain a solid foundation in managing identity, authentication, authorization, and secure connectivity — all within the context of SAP BTP.

What You'll Learn

Throughout the course, you’ll explore topics such as:

  • Understand the core components and functions of SAP Cloud Identity Services.
  • Configure authentication and trust relationships in SAP BTP.
  • Explain the identity lifecycle and its management within cloud environments.
  • Describe risk-based authentication and enable social SSO capabilities.
  • Understand authorization concepts in the context of SAP BTP and SAP Cloud Identity Services.
  • Configure SAP Cloud Connector and implement principal propagation to secure access to on-premise systems.
  • Implement delegated authentication using corporate identity providers and enable identity federation across systems.
  • Apply best practices for managing platform users, business users, and their access within SAP BTP. 

Whether you’re an IT professional, a security expert, or simply looking to expand your cloud security skill set, this course is built to support your success.

Before You Access the E-Learning

This E-Learning is brought to you by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy.

Important: To access the E-Learning, you must complete a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, using your S-User ID.
There is no additional cost involved.

📌Click here for the step-by-step registration guide

👉Once registered, you're ready to begin:

ACCESS NOW


Enhance Your SAP Experience with Value Maps

SAP Enterprise Support offers additional resources to help you drive innovation. SAP Enterprise Support value maps provide expert-led resources, tailored recommendations, and advisory services - all included in your SAP cloud subscription. Get started today: 

  • One-time sign up for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, using a valid S-User ID (please allow up to 2 hours for processing) 
  • Access SAP Enterprise Support value maps for personalized guidance 
  • Request a call with our solution area experts for tailored advice 

Value Maps are part of SAP Enterprise Support’s commitment to your success. Visit the SAP Enterprise Support value maps landing page to explore our range of topics and start engaging with us today.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

About the author:

  • Carol Zhang is a Focus Advisor for SAP Enterprise Support value maps, specializing in the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). Based in Singapore, she has been with SAP for over 20 years, holding various roles across the organization. She now helps businesses accelerate the adoption of SAP BTP tools and services through value map content and SAP Enterprise Support offerings.

Have questions, comments or feedback? Don't hesitate to comment down below or contact @CarolZhang.

entry.title: End-to-End Error Handling with General Splitter and Local Flows
entry.content:
Objective

This blog explains how to handle large data in SAP Cloud Integration using the General Splitter pattern. It shows how to split big messages, call OData services in parallel, and gather results. It also covers error handling using a separate iFlow and how to send error alerts via email. The goal is to build a scalable, reliable, and easy-to-maintain integration flow.

 

Step 1 :-

 

  • This iFlow handles multiple ProductIdentifiers by splitting an incoming XML payload, making parallel OData GET calls, and gathering the results for response.

1. The HTTPS sender is set to /Products/Details with user role ESBMessaging.send and CSRF protection turned on to keep the connection secure.

2. The General Splitter is set to split the message using XPath //productIdentifier, processing 2 items at a time with streaming enabled and exception handling turned on.

3. A Content Modifier is used to create a message header named productIdentifier, extracting its value from the XML payload using the XPath //productIdentifier.

4. The Request Reply is used to call the OData service using the productIdentifier from each message part.

5. The OData connection is set to call the service endpoint hosted on SAP BTP with no authentication, using an internet proxy and reusing the connection for better performance.

6. In the Processing tab, we use a GET request to fetch product details from the Products path. It filters the data using the productIdentifier from the header and selects only the needed fields to keep the response light.

7. Next → Aggregate similar XML messages seamlessly using the Gather step with the Combine strategy to create a unified output.

8. Save and deploy the artifact. After deployment, copy the endpoint URL.

9. Open Postman, provide the authentication details and request body, then click 'Send' to see the result.

*  This is product data used in the body of the request.

 

<?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8'?>
<root>
<record>
<Products>
<productIdentifier>HT-2000</productIdentifier>
<productIdentifier>HT-1138</productIdentifier>
<productIdentifier>HT-1137</productIdentifier>
<productIdentifier>HT-1022</productIdentifier>
<productIdentifier>HT-1111</productIdentifier>
<productIdentifier>HT-1092</productIdentifier>
<productIdentifier>HT-2026</productIdentifier>
<productIdentifier>HT-6131</productIdentifier>
<productIdentifier>HT-7030</productIdentifier>
<productIdentifier>HT-6110</productIdentifier>
<productIdentifier>HT-6121</productIdentifier>
<productIdentifier>HT-1021</productIdentifier>
<productIdentifier>HT-6100</productIdentifier>
<productIdentifier>HT-7000</productIdentifier>
<productIdentifier>HT-1036</productIdentifier>
</Products>
</record>
</root>
 
10. Enable Trace from the iFlow settings in "Manage Integration Content" and redeploy the iFlow. Then re-run the message and open Message Content to view payloads at each step.

 

Step 2 :-

 

  • Now we can try to split the products, enrich each via OData call, gather the results, and handle exceptions using ProcessDirect to an error handler.

1. Add an Exception Subprocess, and inside the Exception Subprocess, use a Process Call step to call a Local Integration Process. Then, open the Process Call step and select the desired Local Integration Process.

2. In a Local Integration Process, use a Request-Reply step to synchronously call another flow via its ProcessDirect address and fetch the response.

 

3. ProcessDirect address format:
    /<PackageName>/<FlowName>

     Example: /myPackage/myFlow

 

Step 3 :-

 

  • This SAP integration flow uses a Content Modifier and Groovy Script to catch and log errors, then sends an email notification using the Mail adapter.

 

 

1. To connect to another iFlow using ProcessDirect, give the address as /PackageName/Current ArtifactName—this tells CPI which flow to trigger.

2. The Content Modifier configures message headers to extract InterfaceId from the payload and stacktrace into ErrorAttachment. This prepares error details for the subsequent Groovy script to create logs and for the Mail adapter to send exception notifications.

3. The Content Modifier's "Exchange Property" tab creates properties like Tenant_Name, IFlow_Name, Message_ID, Time, and Date. These properties, extracted from constants or the message payload, capture crucial context for logging and notification in the exception handling flow.

4. This Content Modifier's "Message Body" tab generates a JSON payload for an incident management system like ServiceNow. It dynamically populates the incident's description with the error's stack trace from the ErrorAttachment header, ensuring detailed error reporting.

* Message Body :- 

{
"active": true,
"activity_due": "2019-04-11T10:42:45.943Z",
"assigned_to": "Beth Anglin",
"assignment_group": "Database",
"business_duration": "2019-04-11T10:42:45.943Z",
"business_service": "Blackberry",
"calendar_duration": "2019-04-11T10:42:45.943Z",
"caller_id": "Allyson Gillispie",
"category": "Database",
"contact_type": "Phone",
"description": "${header.ErrorAttachment}",
"due_date": "2019-04-11T10:42:45.943Z",
"escalation": 0,
"expected_start": "2019-04-11T10:42:45.943Z",
"impact": "3-Low",
"knowledge": true,
"made_sla": true,
"opened_at": "2019-04-11T10:42:45.943Z",
"opened_by": "John Snow",
"priority": "5-Planning",
"severity": "High",
"short_description": "${property.Exception_Message}",
"state": "In Progress",
"sys_mod_count": 0,
"time_worked": null,
"urgency": "3-Low",
"work_end": null,
"work_start": null
}

5. In the GS_CreateLogs_File step, a Groovy script takes the message content and saves it as a plain text attachment in the CPI logs. This helps in debugging by showing what the message looked like when the error happened.

6. In the receiver, we use the Mail adapter to connect to smtp.gmail.com:587 with STARTTLS and plain user/password authentication using a MAIL credential. This allows the CPI flow to send email alerts when an exception occurs.

7. The "Processing" tab of a mail configuration shows email details (From, To, Cc, Subject, HTML body) and one TextPlain attachment named ErrorAttachment.txt from a header source.

8. To simulate an error and test exception handling, we intentionally removed the https: prefix from the OData server URL, triggering a connection failure handled via Process Direct.

9. Now, save and deploy both the main iFlow and the exception handling iFlow, then trigger the flow using Postman to validate the error scenario and check the exception handling logic.

10. The error from the OData call triggered the Exception Subprocess and passed the message to the local flow using Process Direct.

11. The Process Direct successfully routed the message to the exception handling iFlow, where the error was logged, and a notification email was sent.

 

 

●      Final Summary

  • In this blog, we used the General Splitter to handle large data and added error handling using Exception Subprocess and Process Direct.
  • To test the error flow, we removed the https: from the OData URL, which caused the request to fail. This triggered the Exception Subprocess, and the message was sent to another flow using Process Direct.
  • In the exception flow, we captured the error and continued the processing as needed.
  • This setup helps manage errors smoothly and keeps the integration flow clean and reliable.

entry.title: Re: "Delivery completed" indicator causes approved Purchase order to be re-sent for approv
entry.content:
Hello Ankush,

Below are my findings on this issue. Please note it's based on my particular settings, which might be different in your system. 

1. No restart conditions for workflow are configured in SAP (neither in GUI customizing path SPRO or t-code SWDD / SWDD_SCENARIO, nor in Fiori). When these are maintained, system only triggers restart of the Purchase order approval when those fields that are defined as restart conditions are changed or updated.

Another option would be to have BADI MMPUR_PO_WORKFLOW_RESTART in place (with defined restart conditions), but it's not our case either.

2. Considering this, I had to focus on the change history of various Purchase orders instead, to see whether I could find any pattern regarding to when Purchase order's approval is restarted and when it's not.

3. I analyzed a change log of different Purchase orders, looking at both Header and Item changes, specifically for those document types that have Flexible workflow in place + Account assignment category (mostly "K" Cost center). There was indeed one pattern that deserves attention:

  • When Purchase order is approved by approver (in change log of Header changes it has processing status "05" = Approved successfully) and later "Delivery completed" indicator is marked (either manually in ME22N or in MASS), Purchase order's approval workflow is NOT restarted.

 

  • When Purchase order is rejected by approver (in change log of Header changes it has processing status "08" = Rejected) and later "Delivery completed" indicator is marked (either manually in ME22N or in MASS), Purchase order's approval workflow IS restarted.

 

  • When any other field is changed or updated in the approved or rejected Purchase order (f.e. Purchasing group, Purchasing organization, Net price, Quantity), Purchase order's approval workflow IS restarted, which can be considered as standard system behavior.

I believe we can then assume that when we do a mass update of "Delivery completed" indicator for Purchase orders, those that were originally rejected will need to be approved again (their processing status will automatically change from "08" = Rejected to "03" = Ready for processing). I can't, however, answer the question why is it happening (mostly due to the fact that no specific restart conditions are maintained anywhere in our SAP).

In this case I suggest to make sure that before mass update of the Purchase orders one makes sure there are no documents with processing status "08" - Rejected. The workaround might be to block the Purchase order item(s) instead of using the "Delivery completed" indicator. 

Thank you.

entry.title: Handling Connectivity and Recoverable Errors in SAP CPI with JMS Queues
entry.content:
Introduction:

In SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI), custom retry mechanisms handle transient errors (e.g., network issues, HTTP 503) while avoiding retries for persistent errors (e.g., mapping issues). Below is a concise overview of retry approaches and configurations.

Retry Mechanisms
1. JMS Queues
- Stores failed messages in queues for retries.
- Setup: Enable JMS (Enterprise Edition). Use JMS receiver to store messages, JMS sender to poll (e.g., every 10 mins). Set max 5 retries, exponential backoff. Move to dead-letter queue or notify after max retries.
- Use Case: Retry HTTP 503 errors.


- Pros: Reliable, visible in Queue Monitor.
- Cons: 10 GB limit, polling may pause.
- Example: Failed message retried 5 times, then emailed.

Architecture/Design:

Implementation:

This is the Three layered architecture the first layer is Publisher layer 2nd is Transform layer and 3rd is Receiver layer
Layer - 1 : Publisher layer :

In this layer i am hitting the sap iflow from the postman with xml payload and later i am connecting it to the 2nd layer using Process Direct
Iflow:

Postman Payload:

<root>
<productIdentifier>HT-2000</productIdentifier>
<Exception_Details>
<JMSQueue>jmsretry</JMSQueue>
<SAP_ApplicationID>01</SAP_ApplicationID>
</Exception_Details>
</root>

Step 1:

Content modifier: add headers and properties as shown in the below :

Step 2: Transform to layer 2 using process direct Adapter as shown below :

step 3:

Exception sub process:

Here if any error occurs the exception subprocess got triggers and here i differentiated the connectivity errors or recoverable or transient errors like 400,401,500,501 any server down errors and non-recoverable errors like Business Logic errors like syntax errors . If recoverable error occurs it went for the retry else it triggers the mail saying that you need to manually resolve the error

step 4:

Local integration process:

Here i used 2 Local Integration process one for the retry for calling layer 4 and another for the Triggering Email if any business logic /non recoverable error happens.

This one is for global retry:

In the content modifier property i am defining the AppID

This one is for Mail Triggering:

Layer-2 :

In this layer i am getting the data from OData services(Northwind OData URL) using Request-Reply Adapter and sending the request to the third layer using process direct adapter
Iflow:

step 1:

content modifier: define headers and properties here and give the xml code in the content modifier body as shown below:

step-2:

configure the OData adapter as shown below - here i took Northwind OData URL-(https://services.odata.org/(S(xjqjf2rkzrvecxualo4rv4hp))/V2/OData/OData.svc) or you can use this (https://services.odata.org/NorthWind/Northwind.svc/)

step 3 :

Exception subprocess:

Here if any error occurs the exception subprocess got triggers and here i differentiated the connectivity errors or recoverable or transient errors like 400,401,500,501 any server down errors and non-recoverable errors like Business Logic errors like syntax errors . If recoverable error occurs it went for the retry else it triggers the mail saying that you need to manually resolve the error

content modifier:

Router:

step 4:

Local integration process:

Here i used 2 Local Integration process one for the retry for calling layer 4 and another for the Triggering Email if any business logic /non recoverable error happens.

This one is for global retry:

In the content modifier property i am defining the AppID

This is for mail triggering if any non-recoverable/business logic error happens:

Layer 3:

In this layer i am sending the data either to the soap or OData using HTTP adapter for the final destination 

 

Step 1 :

Content Modifier : define the headers and properties as shown below :

body xml code:

<root>
<productIdentifier>${property.productIdentifier}</productIdentifier>
<Exception_Details>
<JMSQueue>${property.JMSQueue}</JMSQueue>
<SAP_ApplicationID>${property.SAP_ApplicationID}</SAP_ApplicationID>
</Exception_Details>
</root>

step 2:

Exception subprocess:

step 3: Router setup :

step-4:

Local integration process:

Here i used 2 Local Integration process one for the retry for calling layer 4 and another for the Triggering Email if any business logic /non recoverable error happens.

This one is for global retry:

In the content modifier property i am defining the AppID

This is for mail triggering if any non-recoverable/business logic error happens:

Layer -4:

This is the global message retry layer in which if any Connectivity error or transient error or recoverable error happens like (400,500,internal server errors) in the any of the first 3 layers this layer is being hit through the process direct adapter 

Iflow :

Step 1:

Content Modifier: 

Declare the headers ,body and properties as for the given below 

step-2:

Configure the JMS for storing the payload that need to hit for the corresponding layer as shown in the below

Layer 5:

In this layer it takes the payload and and ifow name from the value mapping and route to the target layer(In which error happens) it automatically triggers to that particular iflow

step 1: In the groovy script i am Assigning the iflow name what comes from the value mapping


Groovy Script code :

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import com.sap.it.api.ITApiFactory
import com.sap.it.api.mapping.ValueMappingApi
def Message processData(Message message) {
       def map = message.getProperties();
       InterfaceId = map.get("SAP_ApplicationID");
       def valueMapApi = ITApiFactory.getService(ValueMappingApi.class, null)
       def iFlowName = valueMapApi.getMappedValue('CPI', 'AppID', InterfaceId, 'IS', 'Iflow');
       message.setProperty("InterfaceName", iFlowName);
       return message;
}

Layer 7 :

Value Mapping :  To assign the iflow name in which the error happens dynamically we use the value mapping and later this can used by the layer 6 to dynamically route to that layer automatically

Here the AppID are assigned through the content modifiers in the Local Integration process in which error happens and the iflow,IS are the complete iflow address (/package_name / Ifow_name) are declared in the value mapping

Email triggering:

If any non recoverable/business logic errors occurs this ifow hits and triggers mail to the developers with iflow name type of error and error log 
Iflow :

step 1:

Content Modifier: Here i will declare the all the attributes like ifowname ,msg_id,time ,date,exception_message by which the error got created

Step 2: Add the groovy to attach exception message in the mail

This groovy is used to return the message

Mail Adapter Setup:

Fill the all fields and setup/configure the mail adapter before using it you can use this referral blog for mail setup -(Configure SAP CPI Mail Adapter with Outlook SMTP u... - SAP Community)

Test:

The Error mail looks like this if any non-recoverable error/business logic error occurs

entry.title: Deployment App in SAP CALM to Automate imports in Target Tenants
entry.content:
Import Strategy along with scheduled import jobs helps to automate the import of TR in subsequent target system (Quality, Pre-Production, Production) based on the status of the Change Document in Change Request Management Functionality of Solution Manager 7.2.

Similar kind of functionality is now available in SAP CALM which can be achieved using Deployment App.

Step 1: Open your SAP CALM tenant

Step 2: Go to Implementation section

Step 3: Click on the Deployment App

Step 4: Click on create

Give the required details to create a Deployment Schedule 

  • Title – Name the Deployment
  • Deployment plan – Enter the Deployment Plan for which the Deployment Schedule is required
  • Release – Release of the Deployment Plan for which we need the Deployment Schedule
  • Target Tenant – Target tenants include quality assurance, pre-production, and production systems from a system group, which are assigned to the project via the deployment plan
  • Start Time – Date and time of the Schedule
  • Repeat Schedule – On selecting the repeat schedule checkbox, the Schedule will repeat itself based on the frequency provided
  • Frequency – Enter the frequency of deployment (This field is available in case of Repeat schedule only)
  • Feature Status – Enter the status of feature for deployment schedules to deploy changes to quality, pre-production or production systems.

The central entity of the Deployment app is the deployment plan.

With the Deployment app, you deploy features assigned to your deployment plan by creating feature status and/or release dependent deployment schedules to deploy changes to quality, pre-production or production systems.

 
Note: This is not possible for ATO and CTMS-managed transports.
 
We can set deployment schedules as one-time or recurring based on the requirement. We can have a customized frequency in case of recurring deployment schedule.

Click on create and your Deployment Schedule will be created.

After the Deployment Schedule is created it looks as below screenshot 

As per the above screenshot, every 15 mins this deployment schedule will look for the Features using the Deployment plan and see if the status is in Successfully Tested and the release assigned. If the required criteria is matched, then it will import the changes in the target tenant.

entry.title: BO Interface APIs : An alternative to BAPIs and traditional FMs
entry.content:
Objective:

We would be needing to create or update master/transaction data in S4 system. In our example, we need to update line items of Purchase Order using code. 

Approach: 

Initially, the plan was to use the BAPI 'BAPI_PO_CHANGE'  which is a proven method to update Purchase Order. We will be currently using an alternative approach of using BO interfaces for updating the line items of PO.

What are BO Interfaces?

SAP has provided many Fiori apps which are built by using Business Object underneath. These Business Objects contain the paradigm to update its relevant data. They have their own entities using which we can create or change the data.

Why do we need to user BO Interfaces?

Even though there are proven BAPIs or FMs available to do the job, still SAP has provided BO interfaces. They have a few advantages as shown below:

1. SAP is adding new functionalities to its product periodically. A few of the modules (e.g. Treasury) have Fiori apps which do not have an SAP GUI transaction as alternative (When building a custom solution). As these have been implemented completely in Fiori, we may or may not have FMs to interact with database. In such cases, BO interfaces will be a boon for us to interact with the DB rather than we debugging and copying standard code to replicate the same functionality. 

2. Some of the FMs will require us to call a COMMIT statement to save the data to DB. When we are building a Fiori app using RAP, we will not be able to call COMMIT work in our behavior class. (We use RFC calls to update data and call COMMIT which a workaround). These statements do not require a COMMIT when called from behavior class.

How do we find BO Interfaces?

The following steps can be used to see if there is any standard BO interface available for our need.

1. As a first step, we need to visit https://api.sap.com/ and go 'Categories' tab in which you will be able to see the tile for Business Object Interface:

2. Once you get into the tile, you'll be able to see a list of released BO interfaces provided by SAP. As of now, there are 348 BO Interfaces provided by standard.

3. For our scenario, we need to see if there is a BO interface to create or change Purchase Order. Type 'Purchase Order' in the search and hit enter upon which you'll be able to see all the results.

4. The relevant BO Interface is visible as a first tile for us. If you a closer look at the tile, it will tell us the capabilities of the BO interface. Ours' is capable of CRUD operation of Purchase Orders.

5. Once you click the tile, you will be taken to the details screen of the selected BO interface. The information such as underlying CDS view entities, Released state, Operations, and the associated entities will be visible.

6. We are clicking on create of Purchase Order projection. We should be able to see the authorization objects and a sample request which says how the data needs to be passed to the BO Interface.

What are the prerequisites for using BO Interfaces?

 We can classify it into two:

1. System requirement

We would be needing an S4 system to use these BO Interfaces.

2. Developer requirement

We need to familiar with EML (Entity Manipulation Language) which is needed for performing the actions using the BO Interfaces. 

How to use BO Interfaces in our code?

I have created a sample SE38 report program to update Purchase Order. (This report program contains only the core logic required for this article)

1. We are fetching the data from the CDS entity I_PURCHASEORDERITEMTP_2. I am using the CDS view as I am familiar with it. You can do the same by doing a simple SELECT to EKPO table.

2. We are using an EML statement to update a field in PO Line item.  In the below code, we need to pass the keys and the value to be updated. The field relevant for update is set using if_abap_behv=>mk-on. You can also additionally add parameters of FAILED to see if the data got updated successfully. (You'll be familiar with it once you go through EML)

COMMIT ENTITIES is required as EML is called outside behavior class.

3. We are again doing a fetch to see if the data got updated successfully.

4. The entire set of code looks like below.

5. When executed via debugger, the variables in the right shows the old value and the updated value.

Note:

Similar set of code can be used to update the custom field. There are 2 pre-requisite for updating the custom field using the above approach

1. The field should be added via Custom Fields app. The fields that are directly added to DB table via APPEND structures will not be visible as the field is not added to the underlying CDS entities.

2. Fields added via Custom fields app needs the setting to be enabled. You need to enable usage for the field in the CDS entity. This can be done when creating the custom field.

3. If a custom RAP based Fiori app is built, we can leverage its BO just like standard to use its functionality like how we used the standard in our above example.

Feel free to add to the article, via comments.

entry.title: How to provision from Datasphere to IAS?
entry.content:
Dear all,

I have the following use case: I want to provision all existing users and roles from Datasphere to our IAS. I have already taken into account the guidance from [SAP Note 3559583](https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3559583). User provisioning works successfully; however, I am unable to convert the roles into groups in IAS and assign the corresponding members to those groups. In the provisioning log, I can also see the role names as a group attribute. From experience, I know that this setup works, for example, with EntraID. Has anyone worked on a similar case or has an idea what might be missing? I would appreciate any suggestions.

Thank you!

entry.title: SD Sales schedule agreements VA31 automatic creation of delivery dates
entry.content:
How would you organize this process?

Office staff would like to create scheduling agreements.
For example, the same articles should always be delivered automatically on Mondays within a validity period.
Example: Hospital receives new food every Monday.

Variant 1: Create VA31 delivery schedules manually -> but this is a lot of work.
Such a button “Generate delivery schedule automatically until the end of the validity period”

Variant 2: Customer sends via EDI ?
Variant 3: FIORI “Import Sales Contract” with Excel.

Is there another variant?

Thank you!

entry.title: GST India: Input Service Distribution solution
entry.content:
“Input Service Distributor” means an office of the supplier of goods or services or both which receives tax invoices towards the receipt of input services, including invoices in respect of services liable to tax under sub-section (3) or sub-section (4) of section 9, for or on behalf of distinct persons referred to in section 25, and liable to distribute the input tax credit in respect of such invoices in the manner provided in section 20;

The Government of India, on August 6, 2024, declared that the Input Service Distributor (ISD) mechanism will be made mandatory effective April 1, 2025.

Additionally, the Finance Act, 2024, along with provisions in the Finance Bill, 2025, expanded the scope of ISD to include Input Tax Credit (ITC) on services subject to Reverse Charge Mechanism (RCM). Accordingly, ITC on such RCM services must now be distributed exclusively through the ISD framework.

SAP has delivered solution for ISD functionality. It can handle below scenarios.

  • Distribution of credit from ISD to receiving Business places when vendor invoice is posted at ISD BUPLA
  • Transfer of credit from non-ISD to ISD BUPLA when vendor invoice is posted at non-ISD BUPLA and then distribute the credit from ISD to receiving Business places
    Note: RCM scenario should be handled via transfer of credit method
  • Printing of ISD distribution and credit transfer invoices
  • Reporting of ISD invoice

Key requirements

  • ISD must have a distinct GST registration, separate from regular branch GSTINs even if located at the same address.
  • All recipient GSTINs must belong to the same legal entity (under one PAN).
  • ITC distribution applies only to common input services (e.g. legal ) used across multiple units.
  • Credit must be allocated proportionally to each unit based on turnover.
  • ISD shall separately distribute the amount of ineligible input tax and the amount of eligible input tax credit.
  • Credit distribution must be documented via ISD invoices and reported monthly in Form GSTR-6.
  • No further re-distribution allowed, branches receiving ISD credit cannot re-distribute it to others.
  • Distribution formula:
    C1 = (T1 / T ) * C
    C = Amount of credit to be distributed
    T1 = Turnover in a state of a recipient during the relevant period
    T = Aggregate of turnover during the relevant period of all recipients to whom the common credit is attributable

Notes required for ISD solution:

GST India: ISD Solution : This guided answer contains all the notes related to SAP ISD solution

Configurations

Note: SSCUIs are only applicable for Public Cloud Edition

  1. Map the ISD business place to the receiving business places:

    SPRO > Financial Accounting (New) > Financial Accounting Global Settings (New) > Tax on Sales/Purchases > Basic Settings > India > Goods and Services Tax > Input Service Distributor > Map ISD Business Places to Receiving Business Places.

    Or, Go to tcode – SM30, View - J_1IG_ISD_MAP_V, SSCUI - 101911
  2. Maintain Turnover for Receiving Business Places: (Fiscal year will be picked based on Company code fiscal year variant)

    SPRO > Financial Accounting (New) > Financial Accounting Global Settings (New) > Tax on Sales/Purchases > Basic Settings > India > Goods and Services Tax > Input Service Distributor > Maintain Turnover for Receiving Business Places

    Or, Go to tcode – SM30, View - J_1IG_ISD_TN_V, SSCUI - 101912
  3. Map the tax G/L accounts to receiving business places (Maintain dummy tax code)

    SPRO > Financial Accounting (New) > Financial Accounting Global Settings (New) > Tax on Sales/Purchases > Basic Settings > India > Goods and Services Tax > Input Service Distributor > Configure ISD Invoice Posting Settings

    Or, Go to tcode – SM30, View - J_1IG_ISD_POST_V, SSCUI - 101927
  4. Maintain credit transfer configurations, go through KBA 3573453 for more details on the fields required here.

    SPRO > Financial Accounting (New) > Financial Accounting Global Settings (New) > Tax on Sales/Purchases > Basic Settings > India > Goods and Services Tax > Input Service Distributor > Maintain Tax Credit Transfer Configuration.

    Or, Go to tcode – SM30, View - J1IG_CT_CONFIG_V, SSCUI - 107062
  5. Maintain document types for tax credit transfer and distribution invoices
    SPRO > Financial Accounting (New) > Financial Accounting Global Settings (New) > Tax on Sales/Purchases > Basic Settings > India > Goods and Services Tax > Input Service Distributor > Specify Document Types for Goods and Services Tax for India

    Or, Go to tcode – SM30, View - J_1IG_DOCTYP_V, SSCUI - 101910
  6. Maintain default tax code (dummy) for Credit transfer invoices
    SPRO > Financial Accounting (New) > Financial Accounting Global Settings (New) > Tax on Sales/Purchases > Basic Settings > India > Goods and Services Tax > General Settings > Maintain Default Tax Code for Process Type

    Or, Go to tcode – SM30, View - J_1IG_PRCTXCODEV, SSCUI - 103810

Process

Credit transfer:

  1. Vendor invoice posted for non-ISD BUPLA – BEL1(Vendor invoice can be posted using MIRO/ FB60).
  2. Post credit transfer invoices using tcode - J1IG_ISDN:
    Note: The document number entered in all the options will be accounting document number except for “Display invoice distribution” and “Post invoice distribution” option. Here, you will be entering the invoice number in case of MIRO documents or accounting document number for FB60 documents.

    Create Customer invoice followed by supplier invoice.




    Customer invoice:

    Supplier Invoice:
  3. Display tax credit transfer details. (If the tax credit transfer is incomplete, you must at least create the customer invoice to ensure the accounting document is available when displaying the tax credit transfer)

    Once supplier invoice is created, distribution of credit can be done using the supplier invoice created via tcode – J1IG_ISDN.

Distribution of Credit from ISD to receiving Business places:

Distribution can be done directly if vendor invoice is posted at ISD Business Place.

  1. Display credit distribution:

    Distribution is done to all 4 receiving Business places.

  2. Recreate account distribution: This option can be used if credit needs to be redistributed to specific receiving Business places

    Credit is redistributed to entered receiving Business places only.

  3. Post Invoice distribution:

    Post distribution invoices using "Summarize" button, if there is any error while posting distribution invoice, it will be captured in logs which can be viewed using "log" button.

    Distribution invoice posted for receiving Business places

Reporting

Every ISD must file the following return forms:

  1. Form GSTR-6A: Contains details of all inward supplies made available to the ISD recipient based on GSTR-1 form furnished by the supplier.
  2. Form GSTR-6: Contains details of the tax invoices on which credit has been received i.e. distribution invoices.

SAP has provided Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition on SAP Business Technology Platform to meet compliance requirements. Refer GSTR 6 Report | SAP Help Portal for more details to meet ISD reporting compliance.

Printing of ISD invoices

Tcode - J_1HKORDIN can be used with standard/ Custom form templates for printing of ISD distribution and credit transfer invoices

ISD forms for S4 Core 107 and above releases:

  1. 3605786 - GST India: DDIC Corrections for ISD and Credit Transfer Print Form
  2. 3608554 - GST India: ISD and Credit Transfer Print Forms changes

NOTE: This section will be updated with Consulting notes for lower releases, once notes are released.

Public cloud: Print ISD Forms | SAP Help Portal

Troubleshooting and Support content

  • Input Service Distributor | SAP Help Portal - OP
  • Input Service Distribution | SAP Help Portal - Public cloud
  • GST India: ISD Solution
  • 3585077 - FAQ - Input Service Distribution (ISD) : GST IN - SAP for Me
  • ISD Webinar

NOTE: If you encounter any problems related to ISD functionality, we highly recommend you to first go through the links above for common issues and frequently asked questions. If your issue persists, please submit a ticket using the FI-LOC-MM-IN/FI-LOC-LO-IN component.

entry.title: Running a SAPUI5 App from a Previous Commit Without Affecting Remote Repository
entry.content:
Introduction

          Ever needed to test or run your SAPUI5 application from an earlier commit without disturbing your current branch or affecting the remote repository? Whether it's for debugging, rollback testing, or feature validation, Git provides a safe and clean way to do this — using detached HEAD state and local branches. Here's a step-by-step guide to achieve just that.

 

Step-by-Step: Run an Older Version of Your SAPUI5 App Safely

Step 1: View Commit History

To identify which commit you want to revisit:

git log --oneline

This will display recent commits with their short hash and message. Identify the one that's 4 commits back or use:

git checkout HEAD~4

 

Step 2: Checkout the Older Commit

git checkout <commit-hash>

Or for 4 commits ago:

git checkout HEAD~4

This moves you into a detached HEAD state, a safe mode where changes don’t impact your current branch.

 

Step 3: Run Your SAPUI5 Application

Use your usual development server to run the app:

ui5 serve

or

npm start

Your app now runs exactly as it existed 4 commits ago.

 

Step 4: (Optional) Create a Local Test Branch

If you want to make edits or test features in that older state:

git checkout -b temp-test-branch

This lets you commit and experiment locally — no impact on the remote repo.

 

Step 5: Return to Your Latest Code

Once done, simply switch back to your latest working branch:

git checkout main

 

Why This Approach is Safe?

- No changes are pushed to the remote.

- The main branch and its history remain intact.

- You can safely test, debug, or demo older versions of your app.

- Great for testing fixes without branching off prematurely.

 

Conclusion

          Testing previous versions of your SAPUI5 app doesn't need to be risky. By using Git's detached HEAD mode and optional local branches, you gain full control without affecting your project's main history or remote repo. It’s a clean, safe, and professional way to revisit and test the past.

 

Thank you for taking the time to read this blog!

If you found this helpful, feel free to share your thoughts, feedback, or questions in the comments! Let's keep learning and growing together. Happy coding! 

Hello experts, please feel free to correct me if any information is inaccurate.

entry.title: Re: Why is health monitoring not working for Datasphere?

Hi Kurt,

There is actually no setup step for connecting SAP Datasphere to SAP Cloud ALM. 

if you have subscribed to it in one of your subaccounts, then it automatically gets imported into Cloud ALM and is visible in landscape management. We just went into Health Monitoring se case and toggled the monitoring ON for it. Usually after few mins, the data is visible.

entry.title: Re: Wrong behaviour of cl_gui_alv_grid or wrong usage?
entry.content:
@zeno_rummlerI might have confused you with the simplificated value popup. In this popup I explicitely provide a wrong value to demonstrate the behaviour. In such an explicit selection the user of course should only be presented valid options. Never the less it is common behaviour that a search help may provide data that might be wrong in the given context. 

Therefore I coded a specific check to validate the entered value. No matter where the value comes from - manually entered or by search help - if my validation check is beeing processed and I declare the entered value as incorrect, then the UI should propagate this to the user. This is my only point: the validation routine works in one case: when manually entered, but it does not work when entered by F4-value request.

entry.title: ePPDS configurations
entry.content:
Introduction:
PPDS is a powerful tool in SAP S/4HANA for optimizing production planning and finite scheduling. This blog provides a step-by-step guide to configuring PP/DS to align planning with real-time production constraints and business goals.

Main part - Configurations:
Activate Advanced Planning and Scheduling
To activate the monitor Capacity Utilization app,the Production Scheduling Board app,the MRP app Create Optimal Orders for Shipment
SIMG - Advanced Planning - Basic Settings - Activate Advanced Planning and Scheduling - Activate Advanced Planning and Scheduling

Planning Model / Planning Version - /N/SAPAPO/MVM
It covers all areas of the network chain from the supplier's supplier to the customer's customercan be determined by importing the relevant data objects from source systems into SCM, or it can be created and maintained solely in SCM. /N/SAPAPO/MVM

Settings for CIF transfer - CFC9
Define how the change transfers for materials, vendors and customers are managed through CIF. The change transfer settings for resources needn’t be maintained as it does not require CIF.

You can define the resource type creation in APO.
Settings for Data transfer
Define the transaction data that can be transferred from SAP ERP to SAP APO, and vice versa
SIMG - Advanced Planning - Basic Settings - Settings for Data Transfer

Maintain Number ranges for Orders
Define the number range for the orders for in-house production and external procurement that are created locally in SAP APO
SIMG - Advanced Planning - Global Settings - Maintain Number Ranges for Orders

Maintain Global Parameters and Defaults
Maintain the global parameters and default values for manual and automatic planning in Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling.The system will only use these default values if filed value not specified any values in the master data or the applications.
SIMG - Advanced Planning - Global Settings - Maintain Global Parameters and Defaults
Logging - To log the errors we can keep the this to N
Tick the check box use number range to use the number range which you created and assign it.
Interactive planning will be used in product view - /n/SAPAPO/RRP3 on edit mode
DS Planning board will be used in Detailed scheduling planning board - /n/SAPAPO/CDPS0
Conversion rule can be with ATP or without ATP check
Maintain Planning Procedures
Determines which business transactions should be executed for the object that belongs to the task list or order (for example calling product heuristics or creating planning file entry).
SIMG - Advanced Planning - Maintain Planning Procedures
Mostly standard is enough
Maintain Heuristics
Heuristic is a planning function that executes planning for selected objects (in this way, the focus of planning can be products, orders, operations, resources, or line networks) using a particular algorithm.
SIMG - Advanced Planning - Heuristics - Maintain HeuristicsWe can configure new heuristics if we need.
Maintain Strategy Profile
Define the detailed scheduling strategies for Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling in a strategy profile
SIMG - Advanced Planning - Detailed Scheduling - Maintain Strategy ProfilesMaintain Work Areas
Define which specific resources, products, and orders in the table areas of the resource, product, and order charts can be displayed in the detailed scheduling planning board that you call up directly from the area
SIMG - Advanced Planning - Detailed Scheduling - Maintain Work AreasOn clicking set resource popup will appear. There on clicking choose fields select condition popup will appear. We can add the fields that we need. Later We can give the default values which we need. Then we can use this profile in DSB
Maintain Optimisation Profiles
Specify the control parameters that determine how the system handles orders during optimization
SIMG - Advanced Planning - Detailed Scheduling - Maintain Optimization Profiles
Maintain Overall Profiles
Define the settings for scheduling with the detailed scheduling planning board that you call up directly in the Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling or for the resource planning table
SIMG - Advanced Planning - Detailed Scheduling - Maintain Overall Profiles
This can be used in DSB
Maintain Time Profiles
Define the Planning period, Display period segments for the detailed scheduling planning board, the resource planning table, the production planning run or the evaluation of the planning situation
SIMG - Advanced Planning - Detailed Scheduling - Maintain Time Profiles
This also can be used in DSB to define the time period in which the data to be fetched.
Maintain Scope Profiles
Define the heuristics to be restricted for a product using scope profile assigned in material master. The heuristics algorithms other than from the list are not allowed.
SIMG - Advanced Planning - Detailed Scheduling - Maintain Scope Profile
This can be defined in material master advanced planning view. Its available after 2022.
Define Conversion Rules
Define conversion rule which checks the system executes for the conversion of planned orders and purchase requisitions to production orders or purchase orders
SIMG - Advanced Planning - Transfer to Execution - Define Conversion Rules

This will be used to do ATP check while converting the planned orders to process order.
Maintain Alert Profile
Uses event triggers and alarm conditions established during planning and scheduling to automatically identify problems in the supply chain

Maintain Assignment of Node Type - Maintain assignment of node type - plant

Maintain Planning board profiles - /n/SAPAPO/CDPSC2
Define the technical basic settings for the planning board graphics, various planning board charts, field selection and sorting for the columns in the table section of a chart.
SIMG - Advanced Planning - Detailed Scheduling - Settings for the Detailed Scheduling Planning Board - Maintain Planning Board Profiles
Here the goal is giving a yellow color to the planned order in resource chart which is currently blue in color

At first create a planning board profile by copying the standard SAP001
SIMG - Advanced Planning - Detailed Scheduling - Settings for the Detailed Scheduling Planning Board - Maintain Planning Board Profiles

On clicking enter it will ask you to copyall data. Do it.
Later copy the grapical object OPR_DEF_1 and create your own. This will have the color charaters that can be applied for objects in planning board.

Inside the grapical object select process

Just change the color of the bars for process time

Later go to design table for graphical objects and copy the final confirmation operation object and create your own. This is kind of table that will map the color properties with the objects like Planned orders, Production orders and operations.


Give the fields as below and click enter. Then click field selection and remove the old value and move R/3 object to leftside.


Now choose the planned order as compare value


Later choose your newly created grapical object. Now the yellow color will be displayed in the planned orders.



Now create another mapping for production order with have the previous blue color.


This is the final mapping

after saving it I am chaning the planning board profile in /n/SAPAPO/CDPS0.

Also we can reduce the width of the bar. let us reduce it for setup time


After changing come out of planning baord to sap menu and then go back again. Otherwise changes will not be reflected.

Also we can do the same for production orders also.
Now I need the order quantity to be displayed in the order bars. To display that make the below settings.


After the change reload the planning board and change the planning profile. You can see the quantity in order bar.

Later we can add our planning board profile to the overall profile.
SIMG - Advanced Planning - Detailed Scheduling - Maintain Overall Profiles
Here I am copying the standard overall profile SAP001 and replacing the planning board profile.

The same you can give it in /n/SAPAPO/CDPS0

Conclusion:
I hope this may help somone. Please point out if there are mistakes.

entry.title: HTML Template used in the Service Ticket does not show custom font
entry.content:
Dear all,

I kindly ask for your support regarding a consistency issue in our customer communications. We have created an HTML template to be used as a reply template in our service tickets. This template includes our corporate design elements, such as a specific font type—Segoe UI. When the customer service agents type the email, the placeholder #Text# should add the typed text and show it to the customer.

The issue we are facing is that although Segoe UI displays correctly within C4C when the email is sent, it does not appear as intended in Outlook. Instead, Outlook defaults to a different font (Calibri) when the email is received.

Could you please help me understand why the custom font does not render correctly in Outlook? I am aware that Segoe UI is not part of the font selection list in C4C. Is there a way to override this font list either through the application settings or directly within the HTML template?

If overriding via the HTML template is not feasible, could you advise on alternative solutions to ensure font consistency across all email clients?

Thank you very much for your assistance.

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP Hana Cloud Table access over synonym not working
entry.content:
@breglerj many thanks for your reply.

I have tried that and now the deployment is working properly but when executing the ODATA call I am getting the below error:

"msg":"invalid table name: Could not find table/view REVERSELOGIXSERVICE_ADDRESSES in schema LOG_REVERSE_LOGIX_DB_HDI: line 1 col 206 (at pos 205)",
"message":"invalid table name: Could not find table/view REVERSELOGIXSERVICE_ADDRESSES in schema LOG_REVERSE_LOGIX_DB_HDI: line 1 col 206 (at pos 205)",

So I am assuming that somehow the underlying view associated to the service cannot be resolved properly.

Synonyms file now:

{
"reverse.logix.Addresses": {
"target": {
"schema": "SBX",
"object": "2VS_YY1_SHIPPT_ADDR"
}
}
}

View being created in gen folder after build:

VIEW "ReverseLogixService.Addresses" AS SELECT
"Addresses_0"."ShippingPoint",
"Addresses_0"."AddressID"
FROM "reverse.logix.Addresses" AS "Addresses_0"

Any idea about what can be wrong now? I am tend to think that this scenario cannot be achieved unless all the properties in the backend are UPPER CASE.

Thanks in advance.

Regards,

C.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build Q2 2025 - Release Highlights
entry.content:
Q2 brought a wave of innovations to SAP Build, including improvements across governance, integration and user experience. We also had several announcements in the spotlight at SAP Sapphire 2025 around new AI capabilities, unified pricing, and of course Joule Studio, which lets you create custom Joule skills and AI agents. In case you missed it, check out our roundup of key updates from SAP Sapphire and the announcement of Joule Studio.

If you’re interested in shaping the future of SAP Build, we’ve introduced the SAP Build Discovery Initiative 2025. We’re looking for participants to meet with product teams, test early prototypes and bring your perspective to influence innovations from day one. Find more info here.

Now let’s dive into the Top 10 release innovations from Q2 2025!

Enhanced governance capabilities

1. Empowering business users to maintain rules through an application

In Process Automation projects, business users are often the content owners of decisions and the logic behind business rules. While the SAP Build Lobby is a powerful environment, it can also be challenging to navigate and maintain the content of separate artifacts. 

Now IT and dev teams can give business users access to decisions via a purpose-built application, which is opened in SAP Build Work Zone. By empowering business users to access the logic this way, it helps mitigate risks of unauthorized changes, while also reducing delays around the change management process.

Manage decisions through a purpose-built application

2. Improved governance with approval flows & multi-node project transport

Strong governance frameworks are essential to ensure that your development activities remain secure and compliant. We have now released two features that support greater control and quality assurance:

  • Governance approval flows: This enables administrators to control who is authorized to create or transport projects across tenants or sub-accounts. The approval process is fully customizable, allowing organizations to implement quality gates aligned with their specific governance and compliance requirements.
  • Multi-node project transport: Approvers can now move SAP Build projects (apps, processes, automations) through a controlled Dev -> Test -> Production landscape, with approval steps and quality checks at each stage. This ensures dev teams are moving projects securely and efficiently across tenants.

Governance approval flows

Seamless interoperability and AI-powered integration

3. Importing Business Process Model & Notation (BPMN) to generate process artifacts automatically 

Great news for our automation enthusiasts – it’s now possible to import BPMN processes from other tools into SAP Build! BPMN is the industry standard for defining workflows, decisions, and interactions across systems and teams. 

Now process designers can migrate existing BPMN processes from SAP Signavio, SAP NetWeaver, and IBM to generate process artifacts automatically. This long-awaited transportability helps to speed up the logic creation significantly, as well as acting as the bridge between SAP Build and other process modeling tools. 

To get started with BPMN process imports, see the Help Portal.Import BPMN processes into SAP Build

4. Improved SAP SuccessFactors integration with event discovery

From the SAP Build Lobby, developers can manage and connect SAP and non-SAP systems through the Unified Customer Landscape (UCL). Now in the UCL, developers can discover business events from SAP Cloud Application Event Hub, as well as SAP SuccessFactors solutions. This improved integration supports more relevant scenarios for HR processes, such as employee onboarding and compensation management. 

5. AI grounding in company-specific content for tailored responses

This feature makes it possible to base Joule copilot and other embedded AI capabilities on up-to-date documents and user-created content in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition through the RAG (retrieval augmented generation) method. Rather than giving generic responses, it provides end users with more accurate and domain-specific content.

AI grounding within Joule copilot

6. Joule-based assistant to easily migrate SAP HANA applications to CAP

For simplified innovation across services, the SAP HANA Application Migration Assistant is a new extension available in SAP Build Code. This automates the conversion of XS classic applications into CAP-based services to accelerate modernization and help businesses adopt cloud-native architectures faster.

For more info, see Product Updates for SAP Build Code – June 2025 Edition

Optimized user experience and accessibility across devices

7. SAP Mobile Start as the unified mobile access point for users

SAP Mobile Start addresses the challenge of fragmented access to enterprise applications and information for mobile users. The connection of SAP Mobile Start with SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition ensures that users have consistent and secure access to business applications, tasks, and notifications, regardless of their location or device.

 8. Preview SAPUI5 updates for better UX management

To address the need for more controlled testing, we’ve introduced the ability to preview upcoming SAPUI5 versions in non-productive environments within SAP Build Work Zone. Admins can activate the preview in a staging or dev tenant, validate compatibility and confirm everything works as expected before go-live – ensuring agile development, reduced downtime, and better user experience management.

For more info, read the blog post.

SAPUI5 version preview in SAP Build Work Zone

9. Local Entry Point (LEP) to simplify access to standalone sites

The new Local Entry Point (LEP) concept of SAP Build Work Zone simplifies how SaaS application providers integrate business solutions into SAP BTP. Providers can generate a standalone entry-point site in the consumer’s subaccount, eliminating the extra efforts and complexity required to set up their own instances.

To get started, see the Help Portal.

10. Updates to Mobile Development Kit for enhanced UI, APIs and more

The latest release to SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) has brought several new updates for customers, including the newest version of Mobile Services Client in the Google Play Store and Apple App Store. Among the newest enhancements to UI is support for donut charts, providing a new way to visualize data and emphasize different aspects of datasets.

See all the latest updates: What’s New in Mobile Development Kit client 25.6

Ready to see these in action?

To learn more and see live demos of the latest release highlights, join us for the next What’s New in SAP Build Q2 2025 Webinar! 

👉Register for your preferred time slot:

  • July 17th, 9:00AM – 10:00AM CEST: https://sap.to/60414diDL
  • July 17th, 5:00PM – 6:00PM CEST: https://sap.to/60434diDF

entry.title: SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 2-2025 - Finance Projects
entry.content:
The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative enables you, as an SAP customer or partner, to get early insights into SAP's product developments and directly work with the developers to define and shape future product directions.

How it works
Three times a year, a list of new projects is offered. In a five-weeks-registration phase, you can then register for a first (initial) informative call and decide afterwards, if you would like to participate in the projects.

Register now
The second cycle of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2025 is now live! This cycle features 53 new projects, offering SAP customers and partners the opportunity to get early insights into product developments and actively collaborate with SAP teams to help shape the future of SAP solutions.

Registration is open until July 25, 2025

Learn more about the various Finance-related projects and sign up today!

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • Flexible Cost Allocations using SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Universal Model
  • IT Financial Management Cost Allocation Module in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud
  • Operational Transfer Pricing Optimization in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud
  • Strategic Planning Scenarios in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Universal Model

Finance & Risk

  • AI assisted creation of balance validation rules AND comment proposal
  • Financial Reporting according to IFRS18 in S4HANA Cloud
  • Financial Statements in the New Review Booklets Analytical Application

Public Cloud

  • Actual Liquidity Analysis
  • AI Agent for Open Item Clearing
  • AI-powered chatbot (Joule) for Intelligent Margin Analysis
  • Enhancing Payment Run Experience
  • Price Recommendation in S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition
  • Research: Payment Advice In Existing Business Process

Details on all 53 Projects can be found here or in the following Blog. 

Read Customer Influence and Adoption to learn more about our customer engagement initiatives and customer influence programs. 

Let's shape the future of our Finance solutions together!

We look forward to hearing from you. 

Best regards,

Helen

entry.title: SAP Marketing Cloud - Set Email in Best Record strictly by Prio of Origin (ignoring Permissions)
entry.content:
Dear colleagues,

the standard behavior is described here: 2850695 - SAP Marketing - Contact Best Record Email Address not taken from most reliable Source - SAP for Me

Nevertheless, the Email from Sales Cloud (which is top prio) should always be taken over to the Best record. So the plan was to use Custom Logic via Update Interaction Contact (best record).

See here the slightly modified sample code downloaded from MC:

DATA:
lr_ic_root_new TYPE REF TO cuan_s_ce_ic_rt_badi,
lv_contact_number TYPE cuan_s_ce_ic_rt_badi-record_idx,
lv_index LIKE sy-tabix,
lr_facet_data TYPE REF TO cuan_s_ce_ic_badi,
lt_facet_data TYPE cuan_t_ce_ic_badi,
ls_interaction TYPE cuan_s_ce_ia_badi ##NEEDED,
lr_add_facet TYPE REF TO cuan_s_ce_ic_facet_add_badi,
lt_add_facet TYPE cuan_t_ce_ic_facet_add_badi,
lr_attr_rstrctn TYPE REF TO cuan_s_ce_ic_atrst_badi,
lt_attr_rstrctn TYPE cuan_t_ce_ic_atrst_badi,
lr_mkta TYPE REF TO cuan_s_ce_ic_mkta_badi,
lt_mkta TYPE cuan_t_ce_ic_mkta_badi,
lt_old_ic_root TYPE cuan_t_ce_ic_rt_badi,
lr_old_ic_root TYPE REF TO cuan_s_ce_ic_rt_badi.

* Process all updated interaction contacts.
LOOP AT new_contact_root REFERENCE INTO lr_ic_root_new.

lv_contact_number = lr_ic_root_new->record_idx.
CLEAR: lt_facet_data,
lt_add_facet,
lt_attr_rstrctn,
lt_mkta,
lt_old_ic_root.

* Get common data of main facets of current contact into LT_FACET_DATA.
READ TABLE contact_data WITH KEY record_idx = lv_contact_number TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS BINARY SEARCH.
IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
lv_index = sy-tabix.
DO.
READ TABLE contact_data REFERENCE INTO lr_facet_data INDEX lv_index.
IF sy-subrc EQ 0 AND lr_facet_data->record_idx EQ lv_contact_number.
APPEND lr_facet_data->* TO lt_facet_data.
lv_index = lv_index + 1.
ELSE.
EXIT.
ENDIF.
ENDDO.
SORT lt_facet_data BY id_origin id ASCENDING timestamp DESCENDING .
ENDIF.

* Get imported interaction of current contact into LS_INTERACTION.
READ TABLE interaction WITH KEY record_idx = lv_contact_number INTO ls_interaction BINARY SEARCH.
IF sy-subrc NE 0.
CLEAR ls_interaction.
ENDIF.

* Get additional facets of current contact into LT_ADD_FACET.
READ TABLE additional_facet WITH KEY record_idx = lv_contact_number TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS BINARY SEARCH.
IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
lv_index = sy-tabix.
DO.
READ TABLE additional_facet REFERENCE INTO lr_add_facet INDEX lv_index.
IF sy-subrc EQ 0 AND lr_add_facet->record_idx EQ lv_contact_number.
APPEND lr_add_facet->* TO lt_add_facet.
lv_index = lv_index + 1.
ELSE.
EXIT.
ENDIF.
ENDDO.
SORT lt_add_facet ASCENDING BY id_origin id.
ENDIF.

* Get IC best record attributes' restrictions of current contact into LT_ATTR_RSTRCTN.
READ TABLE attribute_rstrctn WITH KEY record_idx = lv_contact_number TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS BINARY SEARCH.
IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
lv_index = sy-tabix.
DO.
READ TABLE attribute_rstrctn REFERENCE INTO lr_attr_rstrctn INDEX lv_index.
IF sy-subrc EQ 0 AND lr_attr_rstrctn->record_idx EQ lv_contact_number.
APPEND lr_attr_rstrctn->* TO lt_attr_rstrctn.
lv_index = lv_index + 1.
ELSE.
EXIT.
ENDIF.
ENDDO.
SORT lt_attr_rstrctn ASCENDING BY attribute value.
ENDIF.

* Get marketing areas of current contact.
READ TABLE marketing_area WITH KEY record_idx = lv_contact_number TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS BINARY SEARCH.
IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
lv_index = sy-tabix.
DO.
READ TABLE marketing_area REFERENCE INTO lr_mkta INDEX lv_index.
IF sy-subrc EQ 0 AND lr_mkta->record_idx EQ lv_contact_number.
APPEND lr_mkta->* TO lt_mkta.
lv_index = lv_index + 1.
ELSE.
EXIT.
ENDIF.
ENDDO.
SORT lt_mkta ASCENDING BY mkt_area_id.
ENDIF.

* Get old best record(s) of current contact into LT_OLD_IC_ROOT.
READ TABLE old_contact_root WITH KEY record_idx = lv_contact_number TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS BINARY SEARCH.
IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
lv_index = sy-tabix.
DO.
READ TABLE old_contact_root REFERENCE INTO lr_old_ic_root INDEX lv_index.
IF sy-subrc EQ 0 AND lr_old_ic_root->record_idx EQ lv_contact_number.
APPEND lr_old_ic_root->* TO lt_old_ic_root.
lv_index = lv_index + 1.
ELSE.
EXIT.
ENDIF.
ENDDO.
SORT lt_old_ic_root DESCENDING BY validation_status.
ENDIF.

 

* Example: Store in best record preferably the email address of the CRM business partner data.

CLEAR lr_ic_root_new->smtp_addr. " clear the email address of the best record (= IC root record)
READ TABLE lt_add_facet REFERENCE INTO lr_add_facet
WITH KEY id_origin = 'EMAIL'
id_origin_group = 'SAP_C4C_BUPA'.
IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
lr_ic_root_new->smtp_addr = lr_add_facet->id.
ENDIF.

* Example: If there is no email address from CRM business partner data,
* take any other email address with positive opt-in (=marketing permission).

IF lr_ic_root_new->smtp_addr IS INITIAL.
READ TABLE lt_attr_rstrctn REFERENCE INTO lr_attr_rstrctn
WITH KEY attribute = 'SMTP_ADDR'
value_mp_opt_in = 'Y'
value_invalid = abap_false.
IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
lr_ic_root_new->smtp_addr = lr_attr_rstrctn->value.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

ENDLOOP.

 
 
The bold part is modifed but the result of using that custom logic is always that the Email is deleted. So my assumption is that the id_origin_group is not the correct one. We use the "new" standard integration between Sales Cloud v1 and Marketing Cloud via SOAP. So the data is stored with additional origin / id SAP_C4C_BUPA but basically saved unter SAP_MKT_BUPA. Nevertheless this also doesn't work in the code and it's kind of difficult to find the correct values. Inspect Contact is also not displaying the Email from Sales Cloud at all even if it is visible one the Contact's Communication section and the Permission (with updates). 
 
Thank help how to correct the custom logic above or, even better, formulate the logic to take the Email strictly by Prio of Origin without naming explicit orgins in the code. Is this possible with using SOAP integration from Sales Cloud with the resulting data setup.
 
Thank you & best regards,
Aaron

entry.title: Re: Goods Movement Analysis : Consumption Quantity wrong
entry.content:
If I'm not wrong, SAP standard only counts 601 without special stock as consumption.
Movements with special stock type E (sales order stock) are handled separately and not included in standard consumption reports.

entry.title: Re: ATC for AUTHORITY-CHECK
entry.content: ATC checks for AUTHORITY-CHECK in type 1 programs need specific check variants. Make sure your ATC variant includes "Security Checks" and is set to scan report programs too.

entry.title: Re: Does "HANA System Replication" support the replicatio...
entry.content: HANA System Replication requires identical HANA versions on both systems. It doesn't support replication between different S/4HANA application versions, even if HANA DB versions are close.

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA Log Restore Using NetBackup on a Different S...
entry.content:
For Scenario 1, use recover data until log in HANA Studio and uncheck the "initialize" option, this avoids full restore again.
For Scenario 2, use point-in-time recovery and set the desired time (e.g. 11 PM) during the log recovery step.

entry.title: Re: tcode CNMASSCREATE - no error message when loading file

As I know, this is a known issue. CNMASSCREATE doesn't always show clear errors for duplicates.
The Workaround will be to pre-validate Excel before upload or use debug mode/logs to trace issues.

entry.title: You Won't Believe Where ZLLM's Documentation Lives (And How It Got There)
entry.content:
How I Made AI Write Its Own Documentation... In SAP's Standard Format

Spoiler: The AI is already documenting itself in production*.

Hello again! 👋 It's Alice, and I have a confession: I absolutely love writing documentation.

But I hate writing it BY HAND. Especially in SAP's bizarre ITF format - that cryptic markup language designed back when the closest thing to modern markup was SGML, and HTML didn't even exist yet!

(Fun fact: ITF format was actually designed for SAPscript in the early 1980s as part of SAP R/2 - when "markup language" meant something completely different! ^_^)

But here's the thing - ZLLM has incredible documentation. Comprehensive, detailed, and perfectly formatted. And it lives exactly where every SAP developer NOT expects it: behind the standard "Show Documentation" button 😃

 

Plot Twist:

I didn't write a single line of that documentation manually.

ZLLM documented itself.

The Documentation That Writes Itself

Click on any ZLLM class in SE80 or ADT. Hit CTRL+SHIFT+F2 (Show Documentation).

What you'll see isn't your typical sparse ABAP documentation. It's rich, detailed, with examples, architecture diagrams, and usage patterns. All perfectly formatted in SAP's native documentation system.

Here's what makes this magical:

The documentation exists in standard SAP format (ITF), appears in standard SAP locations, but was generated by modern AI and written in human-readable Markdown first.

How I Built The Documentation Factory

Step 1: The Analysis Phase

" ZLLM analyzes the codebase
DATA(lo_analyzer) = zcl_llm_00_step_lazy=>new_from_string(
  iv_usr = 'Analyze this ABAP class and extract:'
           '- Purpose and responsibility'
           '- Public methods and parameters' 
           '- Usage patterns and examples'
           '- Integration points: {T}'
  io_llm = lo_llm
).

Step 2: The Generation Phase

" Generate comprehensive Markdown documentation
DATA(lo_generator) = zcl_llm_00_step_lazy=>new_from_string(
  iv_usr = 'Create technical documentation in Markdown format:'
           '- Overview section with clear purpose'
           '- Method documentation with examples'
           '- Code samples showing real usage'
           '- Integration notes and best practices'
           'Based on analysis: {T}'
  io_llm = lo_llm
).

Step 3: The Integration Magic

" Chain it all together
DATA(lo_doc_flow) = zcl_llm_00_flow_lazy=>new( VALUE #(
  ( lo_analyzer )
  ( lo_generator )
) ).

" Generate docs for entire framework
DATA(lr_result) = lo_doc_flow->exec( REF #( class_metadata ) ).

For the Markdown → ITF conversion and integration with SAP's documentation system, I used another one of my frameworks: XRAY. But that's a story for another article... ^_^

The Real Magic: Production-Ready Documentation

This isn't a toy example. This is live, production documentation that:

✅Appears in standard SAP tools (SE80, ADT, SE11)

✅Follows SAP documentation conventions

✅Updates automaticallywhen code changes

✅Includes real examplesand usage patterns

✅Maintains technical accuracy through AI analysis

### Before vs After:

Traditional SAP Documentation:

* It is a class.

ZLLM-Generated Documentation:

 

Why This Changes a Lot

For SAP Developers:

  • No more "TODO: Add documentation" comments
  • Comprehensive docs appear automatically in familiar places
  • Examples and usage patterns generated from actual code

For Enterprise Teams:

  • Documentation stays current with code changes
  • Consistent format across entire codebase
  • Onboarding new developers becomes trivial

For AI Adoption:

  • Demonstrates practical AI value in daily development
  • Shows integration with existing enterprise tools
  • Proves AI can enhance rather than replace existing workflows

The Recursive Beauty

Here's the beautiful irony: ZLLM used itself to document itself.

The framework that makes enterprise AI integration effortless also made documenting itself effortless. It's AI all the way down.

How You Can Build This

Want to auto-document your own ABAP code? Here's the starter pattern:

METHOD auto_document_class.
  " 1. Extract class metadata
  DATA(lo_metadata) = extract_class_info( iv_class_name ).
  
  " 2. Generate documentation
  DATA(lo_doc_step) = zcl_llm_00_step_lazy=>new_from_string(
    iv_usr = 'Create comprehensive documentation for this ABAP class: {T}'
    io_llm = lo_llm
  ).
  
  " 3. Chain and execute
  DATA(lo_flow) = zcl_llm_00_flow_lazy=>new( VALUE #(
    ( lo_doc_step )
  ) ).
  
  DATA(lv_markdown_doc) = lo_flow->start( REF #( lo_metadata ) )->to_string( ).
  
ENDMETHOD.

The Bigger Picture

This isn't just about documentation. It's about AI that enhances existing enterprise workflows instead of disrupting them.

Traditional AI Integration: "We need new tools, new processes, new training..."

ZLLM Approach: "Your existing tools just got smarter."

The documentation appears exactly where SAP developers (not?) expect it. No new interfaces to learn. No new processes to adopt. Just better, more comprehensive information in familiar places.

What's Next?

This is just one example of ZLLM eating its own dog food. Other production uses:

Code Analysis- Analyzing patterns across the entire ABAP stack

Performance Monitoring- Generating insights from system metrics

Test Generation- Creating unit tests from business requirements

Migration Planning - Analyzing code dependencies for S/4HANA migration

Ready to Document Your Code Automatically?

⭐Explore ZLLM: [github.com/oisee/zllm](https://github.com/oisee/zllm)

Comment below:What part of your ABAP development would you want AI to handle?

🔄Share if you think your SAP team needs this

Try it yourself:

1. Install ZLLM in your development system

2. Run the documentation generator on your classes

3. Watch as comprehensive docs appear in standard SAP locations

4. Never write manual documentation again

About Alice Vinogradova:

Senior Software Engineer with 20+ years in ABAP/SAP. I specialize in making enterprise AI practical, starting with solving our own problems first. If the AI can't document itself, how can it document your business processes?

P.S. Yes, this article was edited by ZLLM too. And the Markdown → ITF conversion? That's handled by my XRAY framework - another story of making SAP development less painful. It's recursive intelligence all the way down.

*production for development - is development.

Tags: #SAP #ABAP #AI #Documentation #TechLeadership #Automation

"The ideal documentation is one that doesn't need to be written because it writes itself."
